entries.shortDescription: Access Moov financial infrastructure documentation and API reference.
entries.curatorNote: Moov provides open-source financial infrastructure. This server exposes their full documentation via a hosted MCP endpoint.

entries.shortDescription: Interact with GitHub repositories, issues, pull requests, and workflows.
entries.curatorNote: The reference implementation from the MCP team. Covers the most common GitHub operations. Requires a PAT with repo scope — use a fine-grained token scoped to specific repos when possible.

envVars.name: GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN
envVars.description: GitHub personal access token with repo scope. Create at github.com/settings/tokens. Prefer fine-grained tokens scoped to specific repositories.

entries.shortDescription: Read, write, and navigate local files and directories with configurable path restrictions.
entries.curatorNote: The standard local filesystem server. You must specify which directories the server is allowed to access as additional arguments — the server will refuse to read or write outside those paths. Scope it as narrowly as possible.

requiredArgs.name: allowed-directory
requiredArgs.description: One or more absolute paths the server is permitted to access. Pass multiple paths as separate arguments.

entries.shortDescription: Execute git operations, read history, and inspect repository state.
entries.curatorNote: The Python-based uvx server from the MCP team. Reads and writes git repos. Pass `--repository /path/to/repo` as an argument after adding to your library, or it will default to the current working directory.

requiredArgs.name: --repository
requiredArgs.description: Absolute path to the git repository to operate on.

entries.shortDescription: Persistent key-value memory store using a knowledge graph for cross-session context.
entries.curatorNote: Gives your AI agent a place to store and retrieve facts across sessions. Uses a local knowledge graph file. Useful for projects where you want the model to remember context between conversations.

entries.shortDescription: Fetch web pages and convert them to Markdown for LLM consumption.
entries.curatorNote: Simple and reliable. Strips boilerplate and returns clean Markdown. Good for reading docs, articles, and pages that don't require JavaScript rendering. For JS-heavy sites use the Puppeteer server instead.

entries.shortDescription: Browser automation and web scraping using Puppeteer with screenshot support.
entries.curatorNote: Full headless Chrome via Puppeteer. Use when Fetch isn't enough — JS-rendered pages, click flows, or screenshots. Heavier to run than Fetch; skip it if static fetching suffices.

entries.shortDescription: Web and local search using the Brave Search API.
entries.curatorNote: Good privacy-respecting web search. Free tier gives 2,000 queries/month which is plenty for personal use. Requires a Brave Search API key — sign up at brave.com/search/api.

envVars.name: BRAVE_API_KEY
envVars.description: Brave Search API key. Free tier available at brave.com/search/api.

entries.shortDescription: Connect to PostgreSQL databases, run queries, and inspect schema.
entries.curatorNote: Connect your AI to a Postgres database. Creates a read-only connection by default. Pass the full connection string including credentials. Be mindful about pointing this at production databases.

entries.shortDescription: Read and query SQLite database files with full SQL support.
entries.curatorNote: Great for local databases and data files. Pass `--db-path /absolute/path/to/database.db` as an argument after adding to your library. The server also supports creating and writing to databases.

requiredArgs.name: --db-path
requiredArgs.description: Absolute path to the SQLite database file to open or create.

entries.shortDescription: Read messages, post to channels, and manage Slack workspace data.
entries.curatorNote: Solid Slack integration from the MCP team. Needs a Slack app with bot token — create one at api.slack.com/apps. Required scopes: channels:read, chat:write, im:read, users:read.

envVars.name: SLACK_BOT_TOKEN
envVars.description: Slack bot token starting with xoxb-. Create a Slack app at api.slack.com/apps and install it to your workspace.

envVars.name: SLACK_TEAM_ID
envVars.description: Your Slack workspace team ID. Found in the workspace URL or via the API.

entries.shortDescription: Search, read, and export files from Google Drive including Docs, Sheets, and Slides.
entries.curatorNote: From the MCP team. Requires OAuth setup — run the server once manually to complete the browser-based auth flow, which stores credentials locally. After that it runs headlessly.

entries.shortDescription: Manage Linear issues, projects, and teams from your AI assistant.
entries.curatorNote: Community-maintained server by jerhadf. Works well for reading and creating issues. Note: this is not the official Linear server — Linear has not released their own MCP server as of this writing.

envVars.name: LINEAR_API_KEY
envVars.description: Linear API key. Generate one at linear.app/settings/api.

entries.shortDescription: Read and write Notion pages, databases, and blocks.
entries.curatorNote: The official first-party Notion MCP server, maintained by the Notion team. Uses the Notion API via an integration token. Create an integration at notion.so/my-integrations and share it with the pages you want to access.

envVars.name: OPENAPI_MCP_HEADERS
envVars.description: JSON object with your Notion integration token: {"Authorization": "Bearer ntn_YOUR_TOKEN", "Notion-Version": "2022-06-28"}. Create an integration at notion.so/my-integrations.

entries.shortDescription: Control smart home devices and query entity states via Home Assistant.
entries.curatorNote: Community server by tevonsb. Works reliably for reading sensor states and controlling devices. Your Home Assistant instance must be reachable from the machine running the server.

envVars.name: HASS_URL
envVars.description: Base URL of your Home Assistant instance, e.g. http://homeassistant.local:8123

envVars.name: HASS_TOKEN
envVars.description: Long-lived access token. Generate one from your Home Assistant profile under Security > Long-Lived Access Tokens.

entries.shortDescription: Manage Docker containers, images, volumes, and networks.
entries.curatorNote: Community server by ckreiling. Gives your AI full Docker daemon access — it can start, stop, and inspect containers. Docker Desktop must be running. Use carefully in shared environments.

entries.shortDescription: Retrieve information from AWS Bedrock Knowledge Bases using RAG.
entries.curatorNote: From the MCP team. Requires an existing AWS Bedrock Knowledge Base — set one up in the AWS console before adding this server. Your IAM credentials need bedrock:Retrieve permission.

envVars.name: AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID
envVars.description: AWS access key ID with bedrock:Retrieve permission.

envVars.name: AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY
envVars.description: AWS secret access key corresponding to the access key ID.

envVars.name: AWS_REGION
envVars.description: AWS region where your Bedrock Knowledge Base is deployed, e.g. us-east-1

entries.shortDescription: Expose Claude Code as an MCP server for agent-to-agent workflows.
entries.curatorNote: The official Anthropic server that turns Claude Code into an MCP endpoint. Enables Claude to spawn sub-agents or delegate tasks to a Claude Code instance. Requires Claude Code to be installed and authenticated.

entries.shortDescription: Neural search engine for finding relevant web content using semantic similarity.
entries.curatorNote: The official first-party server from Exa Labs. Semantic search rather than keyword matching — better for research and finding conceptually similar content. Free tier available; API key required.

envVars.name: EXA_API_KEY
envVars.description: Exa API key. Sign up and generate one at dashboard.exa.ai.

entries.shortDescription: Read, search, and edit Obsidian vault notes via Local REST API.
entries.curatorNote: Requires the Obsidian Local REST API community plugin to be installed and running in Obsidian. Get the API key from the plugin settings panel.

envVars.name: OBSIDIAN_API_KEY
envVars.description: API key from the Obsidian Local REST API plugin settings. Find it in Obsidian → Settings → Local REST API.

entries.shortDescription: Fast hybrid code search for AI agents across local and remote git repos.
entries.curatorNote: From the Semble team. Indexes a repo in ~250ms and answers queries in ~1.5ms using ~98% fewer tokens than grep-and-read. Works with local paths or remote git URLs — no GPU or internet required.

entries.shortDescription: Injects current, version-accurate library docs into LLM context on demand.
entries.curatorNote: From Upstash. Resolves the stale-training-data problem by fetching up-to-date documentation and code examples at query time. Free to use; an API key unlocks higher rate limits.

envVars.name: CONTEXT7_API_KEY
envVars.description: API key from context7.com/dashboard. Optional — enables higher rate limits.

entries.shortDescription: Browser automation via accessibility tree — no vision model needed.
entries.curatorNote: Microsoft's official browser MCP. Uses the page accessibility tree rather than screenshots, so it works with any model. Supports Chromium, Firefox, and WebKit. A fast, capable alternative to Puppeteer.

entries.shortDescription: Structured chain-of-thought reasoning with branching and revision.
entries.curatorNote: From the MCP team. Gives Claude a dedicated tool for multi-step reasoning — useful when you want the model to slow down, revise intermediate steps, and show its work on complex planning or analysis tasks.

entries.shortDescription: Trigger actions across 9,000+ apps via Zapier automations.
entries.curatorNote: Zapier's official remote MCP. Exposes 40,000+ actions — send emails, create tasks, post to Slack, update CRMs — without writing code. Generate your API key at mcp.zapier.com to configure the URL.

envVars.name: ZAPIER_MCP_API_KEY
envVars.description: API key from mcp.zapier.com. Alternatively, embed it directly in the server URL as a path parameter.

entries.shortDescription: Read Figma design context and layout data in AI coding workflows.
entries.curatorNote: Figma's official MCP. Lets AI agents read design files, component structure, and layout intent — so generated code actually matches the design. Requires a Figma personal access token.

envVars.name: FIGMA_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN
envVars.description: Personal access token from figma.com → Settings → Security. Needs read access to the files you want to query.

entries.shortDescription: Web scraping, site crawling, and search with JS rendering.
entries.curatorNote: From the Firecrawl team. Goes further than simple fetch — handles JavaScript-heavy pages, crawls entire sites, and extracts clean markdown. Requires a free API key from firecrawl.dev.

envVars.name: FIRECRAWL_API_KEY
envVars.description: API key from firecrawl.dev. Free tier available with generous limits.

entries.shortDescription: Search the OpenTofu Registry for providers, modules, resources, and data sources.
entries.curatorNote: Officially maintained by the OpenTofu project (Linux Foundation). Gives AI assistants access to registry search and documentation for all 3,900+ providers and 23,600+ modules. No auth or env vars required — the hosted Cloudflare Worker endpoint is always up-to-date. Also available as a local Node.js server via npx @opentofu/opentofu-mcp-server for offline or air-gapped use.
